originalTitle: Ebola vaccine could take nine months as death toll rises further, WHO warns
title: Ebola vaccine could take nine months as death toll rises further, WHO warns
content: In its latest update, the World Health Organization says there have now been 139 suspected deaths and 600 cases.

originalTitle: Why does Ebola keep on occurring in DR Congo?
title: Why does Ebola keep on occurring in DR Congo?
content: Ebola was first discovered in what is now the DR Congo in 1976 and the country is now facing its 17th outbreak.

originalTitle: Sierra Leone becomes latest African country to receive deportees from US
title: People Come Back Home to Sierra Leone!
content:
Hello, Little Friends!

Hello, my wonderful friends in Africa! Today, we have some special news from our dear country, Sierra Leone. Sierra Leone is a beautiful place, full of sunshine and friendly smiles. We love to share stories and learn new things, don't we?

Coming Home to Sierra Leone

Guess what? Some grown-ups have just arrived back in Sierra Leone. They came from a very, very far-away place. It’s a bit like when a friend goes on a long trip and then comes back to play! Nine grown-ups have now landed safely in our home country.

Where Did They Come From?

These grown-ups came all the way from a country called America. Have you heard of America? It is a very big country that is far across the big, blue ocean. Imagine flying in a huge airplane for many, many hours! That's how far America is from us.

Did you know? Sierra Leone is on the west side of Africa! Africa is a giant continent with many different countries, just like a big family!

Why Did They Come Home?

Now, this is an important part. Every country in the world has rules. These rules help everyone live together nicely and safely. Just like in your school, there are rules about playing and learning. When people go to live or work in another country, they need special papers. These papers are like a special pass or a ticket.

Sometimes, people go to another country without having all the right special papers. When this happens, the leaders of that country might ask them to go back to their own home country. This is what happened to these grown-ups. The leaders in America decided it was time for them to return to Sierra Leone because they didn't have all the correct papers to stay there anymore.

Did you know? Freetown is the capital city of Sierra Leone! It is a busy and exciting city right by the ocean.

What Does "Deportees" Mean?

The special word for people who are asked to go back to their home country because they don't have the right papers is "deportees." It just means people who are returning home, often because of rules about living in other countries. It sounds like a big word, but it just means people coming home to their own country.

Helping Our Friends Settle In

When someone comes back home after a long time, it’s always good to welcome them with open arms, isn't it? Our country, Sierra Leone, is now helping these nine grown-ups. They will help them find places to stay and get used to being back home. It's like helping a new student in your class feel welcome and show them where everything is.

Did you know? Airplanes fly very, very high in the sky, even above the clouds! They can travel for thousands of kilometres!

Why Do Countries Have Rules?

Rules are important! They help keep things fair and organised. Imagine if there were no rules for games – it would be very messy, right? It's the same for countries. Rules about who can live and work where help countries manage their communities and make sure everyone is safe and has what they need.

Did you know? Every country has a leader, just like your school has a head teacher! The leader in America who made sure these rules were followed was named Donald Trump.

Community and Kindness

In Sierra Leone, we believe in community. That means looking after each other and helping our neighbours and friends. These grown-ups are part of our big Sierra Leone family. It's important for us to be kind and understand that everyone needs a home where they feel safe and loved. Coming home can sometimes be a little bit difficult, so showing kindness is very helpful.

Did you know? When you travel to another country, you often need a special book called a passport. It has your picture and tells everyone where you are from!

Learning About Our World

It’s exciting to learn about different countries and how people travel. The world is a very big place, full of different people and different ways of life. Even though America is far away, we are all connected. Understanding how things work in other parts of the world helps us understand our own home better too. Let's keep being curious and kind!
summary: Nine grown-ups who lived in America have come back home to Sierra Leone because they did not have the right papers to stay there anymore.
whyMatters: This matters because it reminds us that every country has rules for visitors and that we should always welcome people back home with kindness and help them settle.

content: Execution method has long raised questions about cruel and unusual punishment and botched executions.

title: Rosenberg: Putin enjoys Xi's Chinese welcome but heads home without pipeline deal
content: Russia and China showed they were shoulder-to-shoulder on the world stage, but it became clear there are limits, says the BBC's Russia Editor.

originalTitle: Far-right Israeli minister condemned for taunting handcuffed Gaza flotilla activists
title: Far-right Israeli minister condemned for taunting handcuffed Gaza flotilla activists
content: France and Italy are among the countries that have criticised a video showing Itamar Ben-Gvir taunting dozens of activists detained at an Israeli port.

title: 'We'll go down in history' - Villa's new heroes triumph to end 30-year wait

title: Southampton lose appeal against play-off expulsion over Spygate

originalTitle: Prince William celebrates 'amazing night' as Villa fans party
title: Prince William celebrates 'amazing night' as Villa fans party
